assumptions: all UX-facing wording should be reviewable before public display
claims review must sit between UX wording and customer-visible output
packet routing should stay simple and traceable
risks: unsafe wording may pass into UI without review
UX copy may imply treatment or prevention
rewrites may be missed if routing is inconsistent
next_required_validation: Hermes review before runtime routing activation
primary_user_goal: Ensure customer-facing UX wording is routed through claims-safe review before display.
ux_recommendation: Route all recommendation-facing UX copy through Z-Claims before customer display.
next_design_step: Route recommendation, summary, and checkout-facing UX copy through Z-Claims review before any customer-visible release.
